findings.description: Section headings in PDF are underlined (ABOUT OUR AGENCY, ABOUT THE DIVISION, etc.) but HTML renders them without underline decoration
findings.evidence: PDF shows underlined headings like 'ABOUT OUR AGENCY', 'ABOUT THE DIVISION'; HTML shows these as bold headings without underline

findings.description: The 'Important Dates' heading is rendered as h3 in the HTML, while the other section headings are h2. In the PDF, 'Important Dates:' appears as a bold label similar in weight to other section headers, but it is not underlined like the formal section headings. This is a reasonable structural choice and only a minor inconsistency.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Important Dates:' as bold text. HTML uses <h3> while other sections use <h2>.
